title: feat(core): GovernanceCallbackHandler for tool execution authorization
body_text:
## Summary

Adds `GovernanceCallbackHandler` — a callback handler that enforces deterministic governance policies on tool calls using the existing `on_tool_start` / `raise_error` mechanism. No core changes required.

### What it does

Implements a three-phase authorization pipeline for tool calls:

- **PROPOSE**: Converts each `on_tool_start` invocation into a structured intent object with a SHA-256 content hash
- **DECIDE**: Evaluates the intent against user-defined policy rules — pure function, no LLM involvement, no interpretation ambiguity
- **PROMOTE**: Allows approved calls to proceed normally; raises `ToolExecutionDenied` for denied calls (propagated via `raise_error=True`)

### Policy format

```python
policy = {
    "default": "deny",  # fail-closed
    "rules": [
        {"tools": ["search", "wikipedia"], "verdict": "approve"},
        {"tools": ["shell"], "verdict": "deny"},
        {
            "tools": ["python_repl"],
            "verdict": "approve",
            "constraints": {"blocked_patterns": ["os.system", "subprocess"]},
        },
    ],
}
handler = GovernanceCallbackHandler(policy=policy, witness_path="witness.jsonl")
agent.invoke(inputs, config={"callbacks": [handler]})
```

### Witness logging

Optional hash-chained audit trail (`witness_path` parameter). Each entry links to the previous via SHA-256, making tampering detectable. Includes `verify_witness_log()` utility for independent chain verification.

## Changes

- `libs/core/langchain_core/callbacks/governance.py` — handler implementation
- `libs/core/tests/unit_tests/callbacks/test_governance.py` — 24 unit tests

## Design decisions

- **`raise_error = True` by default** — the handler must be able to block tool execution. This uses the existing `handle_event` exception propagation, requiring zero changes to the callback manager.
- **Fail-closed default** — when no rule matches a tool, the default verdict is `deny`. Unknown tools require explicit policy approval.
- **Static methods for propose/decide** — both phases are pure functions, independently testable without handler instantiation.

## Test plan

- [x] 24 unit tests covering: intent hashing, policy evaluation, constraint matching, exception propagation, witness chain integrity, tamper detection
- [x] Verify no existing tests regress

For a more complete standalone implementation with YAML policy files and adversarial test coverage, see [Governance-Guard](https://github.com/MetaCortex-Dynamics/governance-guard).

> This PR was developed with AI assistance.

entities.name: GovernanceCallbackHandler
entities.description: A new callback handler that enforces deterministic governance policies on tool calls.

entities.name: Tool Execution Authorization
entities.description: The process of controlling which tool calls are allowed to proceed based on defined policies.

entities.name: Tool Calls
entities.description: Invocations of external tools within an agent's execution flow.

entities.name: on_tool_start
entities.description: An existing callback mechanism used to trigger the start of a tool invocation.

entities.name: raise_error
entities.description: An existing mechanism within callback handlers for propagating exceptions.

entities.name: ToolExecutionDenied
entities.description: An exception raised when a tool call is denied by the governance policy.

entities.name: Authorization Pipeline
entities.description: A three-phase process (PROPOSE, DECIDE, PROMOTE) implemented for authorizing tool calls.

entities.name: PROPOSE Phase
entities.description: The first phase of the authorization pipeline, converting tool invocations into structured intent objects with a SHA-256 hash.

entities.name: DECIDE Phase
entities.description: The second phase of the authorization pipeline, evaluating the intent against user-defined policy rules.

entities.name: PROMOTE Phase
entities.description: The third phase of the authorization pipeline, allowing approved calls or raising an error for denied calls.

entities.name: Policy Rules
entities.description: User-defined rules that determine the verdict (approve/deny) for tool calls, including constraints.

entities.name: SHA-256
entities.description: A cryptographic hash function used for content hashing and ensuring audit trail integrity.

entities.name: Witness Logging
entities.description: An optional hash-chained audit trail for tool calls, designed to detect tampering.

entities.name: verify_witness_log()
entities.description: A utility function for independently verifying the integrity of the witness log chain.

entities.name: Callback Manager
entities.description: The core component responsible for managing and invoking callback handlers.

entities.name: Governance-Guard
entities.description: A more complete, standalone implementation of governance with YAML policy files and adversarial test coverage.

entities.name: YAML Policy Files
entities.description: A file format used for defining policies in the Governance-Guard project.

entities.name: Adversarial Test Coverage
entities.description: A testing methodology used in Governance-Guard to ensure robustness against malicious inputs.

entities.name: Python REPL
entities.description: A tool that executes Python code, subject to governance policies.

entities.name: os.system
entities.description: A Python function for executing shell commands, which can be blocked by policies.

entities.name: subprocess
entities.description: A Python module for spawning new processes, which can be blocked by policies.

entities.name: Search Tool
entities.description: An external tool that can be approved by governance policies.

entities.name: Wikipedia Tool
entities.description: An external tool that can be approved by governance policies.

entities.name: Shell Tool
entities.description: An external tool that can be denied by governance policies.
